issues.title: How to use dynamic api keys with openAI
issues.body: I am trying to use Spring AI in a multi-tenant application. The openAI api key needs to be set on a per request basis according to our user context. I think this functionality was added in https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2164 however the [documentation](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/1.0/api/chat/openai-chat.html#_api_key_management) uses the `OpenAiChatClient` class which I cannot find in milestone 7 (appears to be gone after 0.8.1?). How can I set the api key for chat and embedding requests?

comments.body:
Yikes, many mistakes on my part here, apologies.

The docs are wrong, there was never such a class.  The `OpenAiChatAutoConfiguration` is not allowing one to override the use of `SimpleApiKey`.

Unfortunately, the `@Bean` definition that creates `OpenAiChatModel` contains many arguments, but that would seem to be the best way to add in your own implementation of the `ApiKey interface`, by basically reproducing what is here

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/3756e165262f74e11ab12608b9585a4cf31bc6e0/auto-configurations/models/spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-openai/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/model/openai/autoconfigure/OpenAiChatAutoConfiguration.java#L72


 I realize that isn't convenient.   I think that the `OpenAiChatModel` should have some sort `mutate()` method (like in RestClient) so that you can get the autoconfigured one and then change a few details such as the implementation of the `ApiKey` interface used.
Thanks for the reply. A `mutate()` style pre-initialized builder would be just great.
#3037 Should help, we are merging it into RC1. Closing this issue, please reopen if that solution doens't work for you.  Thanks!

issues.title: WatsonxAiApi: the IamAuthenticator inside of WatsonxAiApi only takes into account apikey but NOT baseUrl

comments.body:
Please hold - there is an other method called zenapikey.
I will further investigate.

There are two Auth methods: one for cloud (use the IamAuthenticator) and one for on-prem (using ZenApiKey).
Spring AI watsonx do NOT support on-prom (ZenApiKey).

issues.title: Support for Azure OpenAI Structured Outputs feature
issues.body:
Support for the structured outputs feature in Azure OpenAI ensures that the messages generated by the model adhere to a specified schema.

In OpenAiChatOptions, we can achieve this using:

```java
OpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .responseFormat(new ResponseFormat(ResponseFormat.Type.JSON_SCHEMA, "json_schema_here"))
                .build();
```
https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html#_structured_outputs

However, in AzureOpenAiChatOptions, the only available option is to generate generic JSON using:

```java
AzureOpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .responseFormat(AzureOpenAiResponseFormat.JSON)
                .build();
```

Alternatively, you can instruct the model via a prompt, which is not a reliable approach.

Here is the documentation for the Azure GA API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference#responseformatjsonschema

**Expected Behavior**

```java
AzureOpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .responseFormat(new ResponseFormat(ResponseFormat.Type.JSON_SCHEMA, "json_schema_here"))
                .build();
```

**Current Behavior**

The structured outputs feature in Azure OpenAI cannot be utilized.

```java
AzureOpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .responseFormat(AzureOpenAiResponseFormat.JSON)
                .build();
```

Am I missing something, or is this feature simply not implemented?

comments.body:
There is a PR for it now that will be reviewed, however ,what remains is improving the `BeanOutputConverter` to not append the text to "beg it to return json" and rely on the response format to do the right thing.

Closing this issue as it is a duplicate.   Please reopen if I made a mistake.
